content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662         322-5660            322-5901           322-6441            322-6444

July 1, 1981

George H. Soares
Kahn & Soares
1400 K Street, Suite 211
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: California Kiwifruit Commission
     California Avocado Commission

Dear George:

     As I indicated to you in our telephone conversation last
week, a settlement has been reached in the Western Growers
litigation. As a result, disclosure categories for members
and employees of various agricultural boards, committees and
councils have been drafted and agreed upon for inclusion in the
Conflict of Interest Code for the Department of Food and Agri-
culture. For purposes of financial disclosure, the boards are
divided into the following groups:

     1. Boards which expend over $40,000 annually and which,
in addition to research and advertising functions, also
exercise some regulatory power such as the imposition of
grading standards and inspections, quantity or surplus
control measures, stabilization pools, and the regulation
of unfair trade practices.

     2. Boards which expend over $120,000 annually and which
engage only in research or research and commodity promotion
activities.

     3. Boards which expend over $120,000 annually and which
engage only in commodity promotion activities. [This
category of boards was not in dispute, and the disclosure
remains the same as originally drafted for the Food and
Agriculture Code.]

     4. Boards which expend less than $120,000 and which do
not engage in any regulatory practices. [These boards are
now exempted from disclosure requirements.]

George H. Soares                    -2-                         July 1, 1981


     My understanding is that the Kiwifruit and Avocado Commissions
engage in research and commodity promotion activities and have
annual budgets which exceed $120,000. Therefore, they fall into
group 2 above. The disclosure categories for this group are
now the following:

     1. Investments in any business entity which during the
reporting period:
          a. Was awarded a research contract or engaged in the
delivery of the type of advertising or promotional
services utilized by the Commission; or
          b. Was a grower, handler or processor of the commodity,
subject to the jurisdiction of the Commission.

     2. Interests in real property which during the reporting
period:
          a. Was acquired by, leased, or otherwise used by the
Commission for any consideration; or
          b. Was owned by a business entity subject to an
assessment or fee by the Commission.

     3. Each source of income, including brokerage service
income, provided the income was furnished by, on behalf
of, any person or business entity:
          a. Furnishing real or personal property or services
to the Commission as stated in paragraphs 1 or 2 above,
or any gift from such person; or
          b. Which produces, handles or processes the commodity
which is subject to the jurisdiction of the Commission.

     4. Any management position held in any business entity
which is a grower, handler or processor of the commodity
which is subject to the jurisdiction of the Commission.

     These categories would apply to the position of Commission
member, Executive Director, General Counsel, Consultant and any
other position which involves the making of decisions that can
materially affect financial interests.

     After you have reviewed these categories, please contact me
so we can discuss the inclusion of these categories in the Codes
for the Kiwifruit and Avocado Commissions.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]

                                        Diane Maura Fishburn
                                        Counsel
                                        Legal Division

DMF:gs

sections.question_synthetic: What financial disclosure categories should apply to members and employees of the California Kiwifruit Commission and California Avocado Commission under the Conflict of Interest Code?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Based on a settlement in Western Growers litigation, the Kiwifruit and Avocado Commissions fall into group 2 (boards expending over $120,000 annually engaged in research and commodity promotion), requiring disclosure of investments, real property interests, income sources, and management positions meeting specified criteria.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is informal advice correspondence without standard Q/A section headers; contains specific disclosure category requirements organized by numbered groups and subcategories; settlement context indicates this responds to a prior dispute rather than a direct legal question submission.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What financial disclosure categories should apply to members and employees of the California Kiwifruit Commission and California Avocado Commission under the Conflict of Interest Code?

CONCLUSION: Based on a settlement in Western Growers litigation, the Kiwifruit and Avocado Commissions fall into group 2 (boards expending over $120,000 annually engaged in research and commodity promotion), requiring disclosure of investments, real property interests, income sources, and management positions meeting specified criteria.

embedding.summary: FPPC counsel Diane Maura Fishburn informs attorney George Soares of disclosure categories established through settlement in Western Growers litigation for agricultural commission members and employees, specifically identifying the Kiwifruit and Avocado Commissions and their applicable disclosure requirements.
